data.title: Intelligent Bio Solutions announces pricing of $2.6 million public offering; shares fall
data.description:
• Intelligent Bio Solutions (NASDAQ:INBS) on Thursday announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 1.30 million shares of common stock at $2.00 per share of common stock, for gross proceeds of approximately $2.6 million.
  • In addition, the company has granted the underwriter a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 195,652 shares of its common stock at the public offering price less the underwriting discounts and commissions.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about February 21, 2025.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • INBS -14.39% premarket to $2.26.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Allstate estimates catastrophe losses of $1.07B related to LA wildfires
data.description:
Allstate (NYSE:ALL) estimates catastrophe losses of $1.08B or $849M, after-tax, for the month of January.

Catastrophe losses included three events, with approximately $1.07B related to the California wildfires.

The California wildfire event estimate includes reinsurance reinstatement premiums, an estimated California FAIR Plan assessment and is net of estimated reinsurance recoveries of $1.4B.

JPMorgan analysts previously noted that Travelers (NYSE:TRV), Allstate (NYSE:ALL) and Chubb (CB) are likely to be the most exposed to insured losses from the wildfires.

Chubb (CB) in its recent earnings call said it estimates catastrophe loss of $1.5B net pre-tax from LA wildfires, while Travelers said expects catastrophe losses of $1.7B pre-tax ($1.3B after-tax) related to the January wildfires.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Accor targets 3-5% net unit growth for 2025 amid demand normalization
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Accor SA (OTCPK:ACRFF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Sébastien Bazin highlighted the anticipated growth in international travel, with a 3-5% growth rate expected in 2025. He emphasized the strong recovery in regions like the Middle East (32% growth) and Asia Pacific, with Chinese outbound travel nearing 2019 levels by late 2025. He identified technology, particularly AI, as a key operational focus moving forward.
  • CFO Martine Gerow reported recurring EBITDA of €1.120 billion, up 12% year-over-year, nearing the high end of guidance. She noted a RevPAR increase of 5.8% for Q4 and 5.7% for the full year, exceeding earlier guidance. Net unit growth reached 3.5%, aligning with the 3-4% guidance range. Shareholder returns totaled €686 million in 2024, equating to a 7.5% yield.
  • Jean-Jacques Morin, Deputy CEO, confirmed the launch of the sale of Accor’s 30% stake in AccorInvest with an expected 12-18 month timeline for completion.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated the mid-term goal of 3-5% annual net unit growth, with an expected acceleration in 2025. The churn effect from portfolio upgrades, which impacted 2024, is anticipated to lessen in 2025.
  • RevPAR normalization is expected, with growth converging towards the mid-term target of 3-4% annually. High growth regions such as the Middle East and Southeast Asia remain focal points for expansion.
  • The company reaffirmed its commitment to achieving a 9-12% CAGR in EBITDA from 2023 to 2027.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for 2024 reached €5.606 billion, an 11% increase year-over-year. Management and franchise revenue grew 7%, driven by the Luxury and Lifestyle division, which saw a 19% revenue increase.
  • Recurring free cash flow hit a record €614 million, with a 55% EBITDA cash conversion rate.
  • Luxury and Lifestyle RevPAR grew 11% for the year, with strong contributions from regions like Turkey, Egypt, and UAE.
  • The M&F revenue per room for the Luxury and Lifestyle division was €3,900, representing slight growth year-over-year.

Q&A

  • Sabrina Blanc, Bernstein: Questioned the outlook for AccorInvest’s divestment. Jean-Jacques Morin confirmed high market interest due to the portfolio's unique value, stating the process would be complex given governance and asset considerations.
  • Vicki Stern, Beckley: Inquired about the 2025 growth outlook and net unit growth. Management indicated a higher net unit growth expectation for 2025 compared to 2024, despite the final impact of portfolio churn.
  • Jamie Rollo, Morgan Stanley: Asked about the bridge to 2025 EBITDA given moving parts like incentive fees and holding costs. CFO Martine Gerow noted that the second-half 2024 holding cost increase reflected one-offs and project costs, while residential fees are expected to grow moderately in 2025.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed optimism about Accor's growth strategy but sought clarity on medium-term targets and AccorInvest divestment. Questions reflected a mix of cautious optimism and probing for details, particularly on profitability levers and asset sales.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing resilience and long-term goals. In the Q&A, the tone was measured, addressing analyst concerns directly while affirming strategic commitments.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts were slightly more focused on 2025 growth trajectory and structural changes like the AccorInvest sale.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The EBITDA guidance range was narrowed upwards in Q3 2024 but remained consistent in Q4. Q4 RevPAR growth slightly exceeded Q3’s 5.3%.
  • Q4 highlighted the continued churn impact on net unit growth, a recurring theme from Q3. However, management expressed greater confidence in mitigating this impact moving forward.
  • Analysts in Q4 probed deeper into 2025 targets and strategic initiatives like the AccorInvest divestment, reflecting a shift from short-term performance to medium-term planning.

Risks and Concerns

  • Analysts raised concerns about the potential complexity of the AccorInvest sale process and its timeline.
  • Management highlighted geopolitical and economic fragmentation as ongoing challenges, particularly in Europe.
  • Inflationary pressures and FX impacts were noted as risks, especially in key markets like Brazil and Turkey.
  • Churn from portfolio upgrades remains a short-term concern but is expected to diminish in 2025.

Final Takeaway

Accor ended 2024 on a strong note, reporting robust financial performance and reaffirming its mid-term growth targets. The launch of the AccorInvest divestment and an emphasis on high-growth regions signal a strategic pivot towards unlocking further shareholder value. While churn and economic headwinds pose short-term challenges, management's confidence in operational improvements and market leadership remains evident.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nebius Group reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Nebius Group press release (NASDAQ:NBIS): Q4 Adjusted EBITDA loss in Q4 2024 was $75.5 million and net loss from continuing operations was $136.6 million.
  • Revenue of $37.9M misses by $19.81M, increased 466% year over year, driven primarily by the core AI infrastructure business, which grew 602% year over year.
  • Cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2024, stood at $2,449.6 million on a consolidated basis.
  • Capital expenditures totaled $417.6 million and $808.1 million for the three and twelve months ended December 31, 2024, respectively.

‍

  • Cash outflow from operations amounted to $80.4 million and $319.6 million for three and twelve months ended December 31, 2024, respectively.
  • December 2024 ARR for Nebius was $90 million, below previous guidance.

data.title: Baxter rises as Q4 results exceed guidance
data.description:
Shares of Baxter International (NYSE:BAX) traded higher in the premarket on Thursday after the medical product maker reported Q4 2024 and full-year results surpassing its prior projections.

With the recent sale of its kidney care business complete, the Deerfield, Illinois-based firm reported $2.75B in revenue from continuing operations for Q4, indicating ~1% YoY growth on a reported basis compared to its prior projection of a low single-digit decline.

Baxter’s (NYSE:BAX) overseas sales drove the revenue beat, adding ~$1.24B to the top line with ~1% YoY growth, while its U.S. sales remained flat at about $1.51B.

The company’s adjusted diluted EPS from continuing operations fell ~11% YoY to $0.58, exceeding the original guidance of $0.50 to $0.53 per share thanks to, among other things, a lesser negative impact from Hurricane Helene that hit BAX’s North Cove, NC, production site in September.

For the full year, the company reported $1.89 of adj. earnings per share from cont. operations with ~11% YoY growth on $10.64B of revenue, which indicated a ~3% YoY rise.

Updating on the impact of Hurricane Helene, the company said all 10 production lines at its North Cove production facility that were shut down after the hurricane have resumed operations, and the facility is operating at pre-hurricane levels.

As for the 2025 outlook from cont. operations, Baxter (NYSE:BAX) projects 5%–6% YoY growth in revenue on a reported basis and $2.45–$2.55 of adjusted earnings per share.

Its revenue and adj. earnings guidance for Q1 cont. operations stood at 3%-4% YoY growth on a reported basis and $0.47 to $0.50 of adjusted earnings per share.

data.title: Tesla is cited for workplace safety violations as oversight under the Trump Administration begins
data.description:
Federal regulators cited Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) for violating workplace safety rules at the Gigafactory in Austin, Texas. The U.S. Department of Labor confirmed the findings in a statement provided to Reuters. The department did not disclose the specific nature of Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) workplace-safety failures or if any fines were imposed.

OSHA launched the investigation immediately following the August 1 death of Victor Gomez Sr., an electrician who was working as a contractor at the Tesla (TSLA) plant in Texas. Reports indicate that Gomez was killed while inspecting electrical panels at the site. A lawsuit filed against Tesla (TSLA) alleged negligence by the company because the panel was supposed to be inactive.

"The investigation is closed and citations have been issued," said the department, which oversees the agency that conducted the probe, the U.S. Occupational Safety and Health Administration.

The case against Tesla (TSLA) is one of the first to be resolved during the Trump Administration, amid questions on how the Elon Musk-led company will fare with government investigations and regulatory oversight of its autonomous driving technology.

On Seeking Alpha, Investing Group Leader Victor Dergunov is still bullish on Tesla (TSLA), but thinks the EV stock could see headwinds due to lower than expected sales for its vehicles and other products, which could impact the price negatively, especially during this "current uncertainty filled stage."

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) edged 0.3% higher in premarket trading on Thursday to $361.52 vs. the 52-week range of $138.80 to $488.54.

data.title: Short interest on S&P 500 Industrials dips in January
data.description:
Short sellers cut their bets against the S&P 500 industrial sector stocks in January, with the S&P 500 Industrial ETF (NYSEARCA:XLI) gaining almost 5% during the month.

As of January's end, the average short interest rate in S&P 500 industrials slipped to 2.04%, down 9 bps from 2.13% at the end of December.

The S&P 500's industrial sector (NYSEARCA:XLI) gained 5%, compared to the broader S&P 500, which was up 2.7% in January.

Short interest, which could be an indicator of pessimism, calculates the number of shares sold against the company float. (Short Interest % = Number of Shares Sold Short ÷ Stock Float).

Most shorted stocks:

Southwest Airlines (LUV) - 5.93% vs. 5.65% last month.

Dayforce (DAY) - 5.10% vs 7.31%.

CH Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) - 4.57% VS 4.25%.

Amentum Holdings (AMTM) - 4.55% vs 3.92%.

United Airlines Holding (UAL) - 4.50% vs 6.07%.

Least shorted stocks:

Parker-Hannifin (PH) - 0.81% vs 1.01% last month.

RTX Corporation (RTX) - 0.84% vs 0.88%.

AMETEK (AME) - 0.86% VS 1.12%.

Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) - 0.87% vs 0.79%.

Cummins (CMI) - 0.97% vs 1.34%.

Short interest as per industries:

Within the industry, Software remained the most shorted industry within the industrial sector, with 5.12% short interest at the end of January, compared to 6.83% last month.

Passenger Airlines were the second most shorted industry with a short interest of 5.01% compared to 6.00% at the end of December.

Industrial Conglomerates is the least shorted sector with short interest of 1.15% at the end of January.
